text: I wish there were a single word for the bittersweet feeling of something being almost exactly right but not quite — that specific frustration when you have a memory, an idea, or a solution hovering just at the edge of your grasp and you can feel its shape but cannot pin it down. Something like the opposite of serendipity, but without the negative connotation. Not failure, not close-but-no-cigar, but that particular liminal tension of nearness without arrival. German would probably have invented it already, and honestly "torschlusspanik" and "weltschmerz" make me think they were onto something.
